features.summary: Apple reported Q2 FY2022 revenue of $97.3 billion, up 9% year-over-year, setting March quarter records across most product categories. iPhone revenue grew 5% to $50.6 billion despite supply constraints, Mac revenue increased 15% to $10.4 billion driven by M1-powered devices, and Services reached a record $19.8 billion with 17% growth. The company returned $27 billion to shareholders and authorized an additional $90 billion share repurchase program while raising the dividend 5% to $0.23 per share.
features.management_outlook: Apple expects significant supply constraints of $4-8 billion in Q3 due to COVID-related disruptions in China and ongoing silicon shortages, substantially larger than Q2 impacts. Foreign exchange headwinds are expected to impact growth by nearly 300 basis points, while the Russia sales pause will reduce growth by approximately 150 basis points. Services growth is expected to continue in double digits but decelerate from Q2's 17% rate due to these factors.
features.risks: Primary risks include substantial supply chain disruptions from COVID lockdowns in China's Shanghai corridor and persistent industry-wide silicon shortages affecting most product categories. Inflationary pressures are impacting gross margins and operating expenses, while foreign exchange headwinds from dollar strength pose revenue challenges. Geopolitical tensions from the Ukraine war and potential demand impacts from macroeconomic uncertainty add additional risk factors.
features.key_drivers: Strong customer demand for iPhone 13 family and M1-powered Macs continues to drive growth despite supply constraints. Services momentum is supported by growing installed base reaching all-time highs, increased customer engagement, and 825 million paid subscriptions (up 165 million year-over-year). New product launches including M1 Ultra chip, Mac Studio, and 5G iPhone SE are expanding market reach and driving customer satisfaction scores of 99% for iPhone 13.
